name: ETH 4h Exhaustion Reacceptance Rotation v1
edge_mechanism: a directional stretch fails, value is reaccepted, and price rotates back toward medium balance inside a chop regime
thesis: Cycle 87 explicitly includes mean reversion from exhaustion only when structure reaccepts prior value. ETH 4h remains the cleanest pure-chop lane in the basket, so it is the most structurally honest home for this concept. The last five cycles say dense reclaim/reacceptance mechanics should be preserved; this is the clearest version of that doctrine on ETH.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h reacceptance events still produce sub-threshold trade count or fail to carry back toward balance after costs, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price stretches away from value, cannot sustain that move, reclaims value, and rotates back toward medium balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Pure chop should generate more failed excursions than durable trends. Reacceptance after exhaustion is the cleanest structural proof that the market is returning to balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because it is the clearest pure-chop lane and therefore better suited to value-based mean reversion than trend-capable TAO.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Real directional breaks may still be faded if the regime temporarily escapes chop.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets work but overall expectancy stays thin, tighten the time exit before adding filters.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Frequent modest balance rotations, many first-target captures, low reliance on giant runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h clears moderate density and produces materially better participation than recent sparse chop-owned families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated reacceptance entries that immediately re-break the stretched side.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low relative to sparse continuation ideas

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit pure-chop lane for exhaustion reacceptance rotation.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough reacceptance rotations with positive first-target expectancy.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h still undertrades or the moves remain too shallow after costs.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate-to-high participation with many modest value rotations.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reacceptance logic survives on the transition-heavy primary lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: DOGE 4h preserves the same failed-excursion rotation with acceptable density.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h transition pressure breaks the mean-reversion thesis.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger reacceptance rotations than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Useful control to confirm that the idea belongs primarily on chop lanes.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h still shows failed excursions snapping back to value cleanly.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the concept is too anti-trend there.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger but rarer reacceptance rotations than ETH.

long: Exit remaining 20% on touch of EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on touch of EMA_55 or 4 bars
